title: Fix libseccomp not working in travis
body:
Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

Lets see if this fixes travis ci.

comments.body: Wait, how did this fix it? We run the Travis tests in containers, so why does having libseccomp on the host matter?
@mheon This is failing during the build phase not in the test phase.  Doesn't this happen outside of the container.
@rhatdan I think everything on Travis that is not lint and gofmt should be running in a container

Alright, this definitely fixes Travis, at least.
LGTM. I'm going to trigger Homu and hope it's in better shape than papr.

:pushpin: Commit b7c41dd has been approved by `mheon`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=mheon b7c41dde55aa5b814e3970e0ea8386362be9b154 -->

@jlebon @cevich Any idea what the 

```
Fedora fedora/27/cloud Podman — An internal error occurred. 
```
is all about?

:sunny: Test successful - [status-papr](https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/libpod/9a2e267637beeb535aac08aee99bcf24db5fce61.1525099353527773952/index.html)
Approved by: mheon
Pushing 9a2e267637beeb535aac08aee99bcf24db5fce61 to master...
@rhatdan CI infra has the hiccups this morning.
@rhatdan what jlebon said.  Those status messages come from the underlying tooling, so in this case papr (or it's executor) literally posted that it back to github.
